extraction.one_line_summary: GAVIRIA Fernando won the sprint by using both his own leadout and Bora-Hansgrohe's train after Quick-Step stayed patient in the final 500m
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step positioned both Gaviria and second sprinter deep, allowing Gaviria to poach Bora-Hansgrohe's leadout instead of launching own train early.
Bennett showed sprint form despite Bora losing control, suggesting Quick-Step's dual-threat strategy forced rivals into premature positioning errors.
GC riders Thomas/Landa clustering 2nd-3rd overall on flat island stages reveals Sky's willingness to defer individual ambitions early in Grand Tours.

rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: positive: Won by tactically sitting on rival leadouts rather than relying solely on teammate, showing race intelligence beyond pure speed
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: Placed high despite team losing sprint train control in final 500m, indicating strong individual positioning ability
rider_signals.GREIPEL Andre: negative: Caught far back with 500m remaining despite Bora attempting leadout, suggesting declining ability to maintain front position in technical finishes
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: Not mentioned despite being top-tier sprinter, indicating either poor positioning or mechanical issues in straightforward sprint finish
